description: Address 4 review issues: (1) Add homeState form control to AddCandidateModal, (2) Fix sort coercion of undefined values, (3) Add homeState to fallback dummy data, (4) Vary homeState values across mock candidates for testability.
steps: 1. Add homeState form control to AddCandidateModalComponent's basicInfoForm and add a corresponding mat-form-field in the template.
2. Fix the sort comparison in candidate-list's applyFiltersAndSort() to use (aVal ?? '').toString() instead of String(aVal).
3. Add homeState to each object in getDummyCandidates() in candidate-list.component.ts.
4. Add homeState to each object in getDummyCandidate() in candidate-detail.component.ts.
5. Vary homeState values in mock-onboarding.interceptor.ts across mock candidates instead of all being 'TX'.
acceptance_criteria: The add/edit candidate modal has a Home State text input field
Sorting on optional fields like homeState does not produce 'undefined' string comparisons
Fallback dummy data in candidate-list includes homeState values
Fallback dummy data in candidate-detail includes homeState values
Mock interceptor candidates have varied state abbreviations
verification: Run: cd /projects/sandbox/sri-frontend && NODE_OPTIONS='--max-old-space-size=4096' npx tsc --noEmit --project tsconfig.json 2>&1 | grep -E "candidate-list|add-candidate-modal|mock-onboarding|candidate-detail" | head -20
findings: All 4 review issues resolved. TypeScript compilation passes for all modified files. Pre-existing errors (270) in unrelated spec/story files remain unchanged. homeState field is now properly wired end-to-end: form input -> payload -> model -> display.
